message:
feat(protocol_recovery): Cx-first execute_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds `RecoveryEngine::execute_with_cx` behind the asupersync-runtime
feature, extending the Cx-first migration pattern to the retry engine
that guards mux reconnect / list-panes / RPC call paths.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/protocol_recovery.rs (+192 lines)

   a. Adds `RecoveryError::Cancelled` variant.

      Distinct from `Disabled`, `CircuitOpen`, and
      `RetriesExhausted` — surfaces that the caller's capability
      context was cancelled before or during the retry cycle so
      callers can distinguish operator abort from legitimate
      failure-exhaustion.

   b. Adds `execute_with_cx(cx: &Cx, operation, classify)`.

      Semantics mirror `execute` with two Cx-aware changes:

        * Pre-flight cancellation. If `cx.is_cancel_requested()` is
          true on entry, returns immediately with
          `RecoveryError::Cancelled`. The operation closure is never
          called — an operator who has abandoned the outer scope
          should not trigger a new operation, a circuit-breaker
          state transition, or a permanent-counter increment.

        * Between-attempt backoff bound via
          `crate::runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx`. If the Cx is
          cancelled mid-sleep, surfaces `RecoveryError::Cancelled`
          rather than swallowing cancellation and returning
          `RetriesExhausted` on the next attempt.

      All other semantics (total_operations++, consecutive_permanent
      gate, circuit.allow(), retry accounting, degradation report,
      etc.) are preserved identically.

   Legacy `execute` preserved; strictly additive.

2. Three new tests under the existing `mod tests`:

   * `execute_with_cx_pre_cancelled_short_circuits`: pre-cancels the
     Cx; asserts Cancelled outcome, 0 attempts, empty error_kinds,
     AND that the operation closure is never called (via an AtomicU32
     call counter). A Cx-unaware loop would invoke the operation
     before the first sleep.

   * `execute_with_cx_happy_path_first_try`: live Cx + first-try Ok;
     asserts Ok(value) and first_try_successes counter increments.
     Pins that the Cx entry point doesn't regress the success path.

   * `execute_with_cx_disabled_returns_disabled`: `config.enabled = false`
     + live Cx; asserts Disabled outcome. Pins that Cx doesn't
     accidentally flip the disabled-path semantics.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib protocol_recovery::: 88/88 pass
    (85 existing + 3 new Cx-first)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress. Retry-engine surface now Cx-first
(complements the existing `retry.rs with_retry_cx` Cx-first entry point
from commit f4fbb7d4 — one is generic backoff, this is protocol-aware
retry with circuit breaker).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
